channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.eurogamer.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.eurogamer.net

item.title: Old School RuneScape's latest League is out now, pushing thousands of players to cry at kids and get haircuts for the devil
item.content:encoded:
Old School RuneScape has just launched its sixth ever limited-time league event, allowing MMO lovers to dive into a selection of new servers to experience the world under the thumb of the hellish Yama. Called Demonic Pacts, over 150,000 people are playing the game as of writing, mere hours since its release.

Running from today up until the 10th June, Demonic Pacts locks off the starting zone of Lumbridge and Draynor, and instead thrusts the population into the relatively-new Varlamore. From here, a massive list of tasks lies between powerful new pacts that dramatically change the typical RuneScape gameplay.

With boosted XP rates and infinite run energy, Leagues are a faster-paced approach to the grindy MMO, allowing adventurers to blast through levels wish relative ease. There are also relics, which provide massive boons to your character, such as toothless resource gathering, automatic smelting and cooking, and much more.

The League's namesake - demonic pacts - take the form of a Path of Exile style skill tree. By completing select tasks, you can construct a quirky build that flips the whole game on its head. For example, crossbows always hitting their max damage turns a slower ranged damage type into a walking cannon. These, thankfully, are all respecable, so experimentation is encouraged.

For those who complete these tasks, special trophies and cosmetic unlocks can be brought over into the regular Old School RuneScape servers. Some of these tasks are really daunting, but for today, the majority of them are quite simple. Milking a buffalo, getting a hair cut, crying in front of a child, and picking cabbages are a few select prime picks, and only the tiniest slice of the full pie.

Does this sound interesting to you? Let us know how you feel about Old School RuneScape, and its Leagues. If you've not thought about RuneScape in years - you may be surprised to hear it's actually having a bit of a boom right now.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/old-school-runescapes-demonic-pacts-out-now
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Hades 2's second post-launch update secretly adds a new game mode
item.content:encoded:
Hades 2 is pretty great, but as we all know, developer Supergiant Games rarely steps away from its hits right after dropping a killer 1.0 launch. Now, alongside yesterday's PS5 and Xbox release, the second post-launch patch has arrived.

The patch notes on Steam give a solid idea of what to expect, including a bunch of improvements to romances, new narrative beats, and the ability to give everyone gifts forever. But the shocker comes in the form of an entirely new game mode the developer has kept a secret until players found it themselves.

Dream Dives are described as "a breezy alternative to your regular nightly routine that will always keep you guessing." Hades 2's traditional area progression isn't used in this mode. Instead, four zones are selected in random order while keeping enemy difficulty similar to the regular runs no matter their order of appearance.

Shiny Stars are a new resource you can exchange for "special rewards" and the other materials are gone. Dialogues are also scrapped (even when picking boons), since the mode is built around the idea of getting straight into the action. To access Dream Dives, you need to first complete the quest which has you waking up Hypnos and then lie down on a "dreamy bed" that appears in the Training Grounds.

Icarus will also form bonds with players more easily after the update, as reported by VGC. It's a tweak worth underlining after players complaints about him being difficult to romance. Considering all the changes and additions packed into this patch which directly respond to player feedback, I'd expect similar updates in the future.

Eurogamer's review of Hades 2 called it an "emboldened" evolution of "Supergiant's visionary approach to storytelling and roguelike design."

Hades II

PS5, Xbox Series X/S, PC, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/hades-2-patch-2-adds-secret-game-mode-and-romance-improvements
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Filming resumes on Amazon's Tomb Raider adaptation, after Sophie Turner's back injury halted production last month
item.content:encoded:
Plus, fans think they know where the show's Croft Manor is.

Filming for Amazon's live-action Tomb Raider adaptation has reportedly resumed, after Sophie Turner suffered an injury last month.

Back in March, production was halted on the upcoming series while Turner - who is set to star as Lara Croft in the adaptation - recovered from a back injury. At this time, it was unclear if Turner would be out of action for months or just a matter of weeks.

Thankfully, though, it appears to be the latter, as TMZ reports filming for Tomb Raider has now resumed. It stated the recent pause will not impact the show's production schedule. "With its star out, Tomb Raider production had no choice but to shut down... but we were told the crew was paid the entire time," the publication added.

Along with the resumed filming, some within the Tomb Raider community also believe they have worked out where the series will be basing its take on Lara's not-so-humble abode, Croft Manor. As spotted by Tomb Raider Chronicles, Wrest Park - a Grade I-listed estate in Bedfordshire - was closed to the public earlier this year, as Amazon took over the location for filming purposes, which included fireworks.

While it was not stated that the estate was being closed for Tomb Raider specifically, the residence would certainly be a fitting match for Lara's ancestral home... It's a 19th-century mansion in the style of a French chateau, which sounds right up her alley. The estate has also been used as a filming location for the likes of Downton Abbey: A New Era and Bridgerton.

Filming for the Tomb Raider series - which along with Turner also stars Celia Imrie, Jason Isaacs, Sigourney Weaver and Martin Bobb-Semple - initially began in January. The following month, we got our first look at Turner on set as Lara, as she parachuted into a soggy woodland with two pistols strapped to her thighs. We've since seen the actress on the back of a motorcycle, accompanied by Bobb-Semple's Zip.

Additionally, former Lara Croft actress Keeley Hawes was also spotted on set in London earlier this year, with many suspecting she will play Lara's mother Amelia on the show's release.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/filming-amazon-tomb-raider-adaptation-sophie-turner-back-injury-croft-manor
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
